subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 13 — 2026-07-21 — A Better Ear Is Not a Recovery
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 13

2026-07-21 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/intelligent-brahmagupta-h6ri7y

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (8.17 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: A Better Ear Is Not a Recovery

2 · Paste the post copy

Cook Inlet has about 331 belugas left, and AI can finally hear them through the ship noise.

NOAA Fisheries, Microsoft AI for Good and Alaska Fish and Game built a two stage listener: one model flags a sound, another names the species. A separate NOAA effort is adding satellite eyes.

These 9 slides walk through the machine, the wall of noise it fights, and one honest chart: 331 today, still about three quarters below the 1,300 counted in 1979.

A sharper ear tells managers where and when the belugas are. It does not quiet the ships or refill the prey. So what would it actually take to turn a clearer signal into more whales?

#CookInletBeluga #MarineBioacoustics #AlaskaScience

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources for this deck, primary first.

Marine Mammal Science, June 7 2026, https://doi.org/10.1111/mms.70213
NOAA Fisheries, Cook Inlet beluga whale species page, https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/species/beluga-whale
NOAA Fisheries, satellite monitoring of Cook Inlet belugas, https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/alaska/science-data/satellite-monitoring-cook-inlet-beluga-whales

Honesty note. The acoustic paper's methods here are drawn from its published abstract, and no detection accuracy figure is claimed because none appears in that abstract.

Know this story firsthand? Add context or a correction below.

Deliverable completeness80.1Post copy, 3 hashtags, first-comment sources, title, aftercare present; URL resolution verified on main.

Editor's note

No hard fails; ships at 8.77 vs 8.3. Caveats disclosed in the email: (1) the S3 beluga hero is the designed Canvas fallback because the akthree GPU snapshot sentinel false-negatived on the transparent subject, and it reads flat (the deck's weakest surface). (2) Honesty is clean: no invented detection-accuracy percentage, the 331 count is hedged (may be stabilizing, still about three quarters below 1979), and the S8 boundary box shows noise/prey/habitat with no intake port.

Docket: closing soon

From the Alaska AI Docket, the public tracker this routine maintains daily.

Subscriber alerts sent this run: none due.

Automation changes this run (1)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
engine		AKT.snapshot (the akthree black-frame sentinel) gains a second, purely-additive COVERAGE accept path: after the historic 24-sample mean/variance read it does one full-buffer readPixels and counts strided lit pixels (luminance > litFloor=12), and returns ok=true if EITHER the mean/variance path passes OR the lit-pixel count clears LIT_MIN (max(48, 0.08% of samples)). It now returns {ok, variance, litCount} and accepts opts {litFloor, litMin, stride}. This fixes the silent false-fail where an object hero that fills only part of the frame over a transparent/dark empty background read mean/variance ~0 at the 24 sparse points and was wrongly judged a dead GL frame, forcing the flat Canvas fallback. The dead-canvas contract is preserved: a genuinely black/empty frame has litCount 0 AND fails mean/variance, so it still returns ok=false.
why: Run 2026-07-21 primary reactive scar (chronic flat-hero / artwork-craft=7): S6's akthree GPU beluga composited over a transparent background as a subject filling under ~half the frame; the sentin		revert the upgrade(2026-07-21) commit (AKT.snapshot returns to the 24-sample mean/variance-only sentinel returning {ok, variance})
pending upgrade(2026-07-21) commit (showrunner stages this set)

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post from a personal profile if possible; the analytical voice travels further from a person than a page. Tue to Thu 8 to 11am AKT are strong slots; cadence is your call.
  • Verify the PDF uploaded as a document (carousel) post, not flat images, and that all 9 slides swipe in order.
  • Paste per-slide alt text when you upload so the deck is accessible and ranker-legible.
  • Confirm the 3 hashtags sit at the very end of the caption and no link is in the body.
  • Paste the sources block as the first comment within 60 seconds of posting.
  • Reply to comments in the first 90 minutes; note saves and comments after 48 hours for the ledger.
